brandSub: Protocol · Diplomacy · Public Communication · Security · Communicology
metaDesc: Ethics and AI-use policy for WPA Journal: disclosure, citation integrity and human responsibility.
title: Ethics & AI Use
deck: How WPA Journal handles research ethics and the responsible use of AI tools.
k002: WPA Journal is edited by people. Human editorial responsibility for accuracy, integrity and final decisions is always required and cannot be delegated to automated tools.
k004: AI tools may assist with language, structure or research support. They are aids, not authors. Authors remain fully responsible for the accuracy of all content they submit.
k006: Where AI tools have meaningfully assisted the preparation of a manuscript, this should be disclosed clearly so readers and editors understand how the work was produced.
k008: Fabricated citations, unverified bibliographic data and false attribution of sources are not allowed.
k010: Plagiarism in any form is not accepted. All sources must be properly attributed and verifiable.
k012: Authors should disclose any financial, institutional or personal interests that could be perceived to influence the work.
k013: WPA Journal is an independent institutional publication platform. External indexing, ISSN, DOI and other bibliographic statuses are listed only when officially obtained.
